title: t_hier_block_threads_bad.py --vltmt fails on subsequent runs
body:
I noticed that this test keeps failing on me, but when I clean out the `obj_vltmt` dir it passes again:
```
$ test_regress/t/t_hier_block_threads_bad.py --vltmt | tail -n1
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
==SUMMARY: Passed 1  Failed 0  Time 0:00
==TESTS DONE, PASSED: Passed 1  Failed 0  Time 0:00
$ test_regress/t/t_hier_block_threads_bad.py --vltmt | tail -n1
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
%Warning: vltmt/t_hier_block_threads_bad: Exec of verilator ok, but expected to fail
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
==TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
$ test_regress/t/t_hier_block_threads_bad.py --vltmt | tail -n1
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
%Warning: vltmt/t_hier_block_threads_bad: Exec of verilator ok, but expected to fail
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
==TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
tstrader@rd1fpgadev-tstrader1:/usr/scratch/work/devs/verilator$ rm -rf test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_hier_block_threads_bad/
$ test_regress/t/t_hier_block_threads_bad.py --vltmt | tail -n1
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
==SUMMARY: Passed 1  Failed 0  Time 0:00
==TESTS DONE, PASSED: Passed 1  Failed 0  Time 0:00
```

I think it may be some bad interaction between `--skip-idential` and `--hierarchical` but I haven't investigated any further.

This behavior has existed since the test was added in fabded95df8c60b20de5e19d2389a6ed32dcaaf6

cc @b-chmiel
